news.t: Drone strikes beyond the battlefield pump up market for technology to repel them
Defense contractors would be barred from buying back their stock in bill approved by Senate panel
Trump invokes Defense Production Act for munitions, supply chains
As Europe rearms, 'wingman' aircraft take centre stage
Defense Stocks Are in a Bear Market. How a Peace Deal Can Send Them Higher.
Kongsberg closes Zone 5 acquisition, formalises affordable missile push
US airlines fuel costs soared in April to $6.5 billion
UK defence show RIAT cancelled because of Iran war
Japan's global defense business may be on the cusp of a big breakout
Taiwan parliament approves extra defence spending but less than government wanted

holdings.infoBox: DFEN is an equity ETF with a total of 62 individual holdings. The top holdings are Dreyfus Govt Cash Man Ins stock at 23.80%, GE Aerospace at 14.06%, RTX Corporation at 9.85%, The Boeing Company at 6.13%, and Aerospace & Defense Bull 3x at 4.44%.

overview.description: The Direxion Daily Aerospace & Defense Bull 3X ETF (DFEN) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the DJ US Select / Aerospace & Defense index. The fund provides 3x leveraged exposure to a market-cap- weighted index of US aerospace and defense companies. DFEN was launched on May 3, 2017 and is issued by Direxion.

data.text: The Royal International Air Tattoo, one of ​the world's largest defence ‌shows, has been cancelled due to military operations linked ​to the Iran ​war, it said on Friday.

data.title: The Pentagon is putting industry on a key 'Golden Dome' weapon that military leaders have said might ultimately be too expensive
data.text: Twelve defense companies have been picked for initial work on space-based interceptors for the US' Golden Dome. These interceptors are the costliest and most complex parts of Golden Dome.

data.title: ETF leader board dominated by nimble funds
data.text: The top annual ETF performers share a common trait, the firm behind them and investors who are ready to trade no matter which direction the market goes.
